---
title: "Your Proposal Title Here"
bibliography: references.bib
csl: american-political-science-association.csl
suppress-bibliography: true
---

<!-- PROJECT DESCRIPTION – 15 PAGE MAXIMUM (standard proposals)
     Check your solicitation for specific page limits.
     Includes text, figures, and tables.

     Required elements per NSF PAPPG:
       - Motivation and context
       - Goals and objectives
       - Detailed research plan
       - Expected outcomes and timeline
       - Explicit Intellectual Merit section
       - Explicit Broader Impacts section
       - Results from Prior NSF Support

     Citations appear in-text here. The reference list is printed
     separately via references.qmd (suppress-bibliography: true). -->

# Project Description

## Introduction and Motivation

TODO: Establish the research context and gap your proposal addresses.
Cite relevant literature to demonstrate your command of the field
[@example2024].

## Research Plan

### Aim 1: TODO

TODO: Describe the first research aim. Include objectives, methods,
data sources, analytical approach, and expected outcomes.

### Aim 2: TODO

TODO: Describe the second research aim.

### Aim 3: TODO

TODO: Describe the third research aim.

## Data and Ethical Considerations

TODO: Describe the data you will use, any human subjects considerations,
and how you will address ethical issues.

## Feasibility and Risk Mitigation

TODO: Address potential challenges and your contingency plans.

## Expected Outcomes

TODO: Describe the tangible products (datasets, publications, software)
and their significance.

## Intellectual Merit

TODO: Synthesize how the proposed work advances knowledge. This should
go beyond restating the aims — explain the conceptual contribution.

## Broader Impacts

TODO: Describe impacts on education, infrastructure, broadening
participation, and society. Be specific about activities and mechanisms.

## Timeline and Work Plan

TODO: Provide a timeline with milestones. Consider including a Gantt
chart or milestone table.

<!-- Example milestone table:
| Phase | Activity | Deliverable |
|:------|:---------|:------------|
| Year 1, Q1-Q2 | Data collection | Raw dataset |
| Year 1, Q3-Q4 | Analysis | Draft manuscript |
| Year 2, Q1-Q2 | Validation | Revised analyses |
| Year 2, Q3-Q4 | Dissemination | Published articles |
-->

## Results from Prior NSF Support

TODO: If you have received prior NSF support, describe the results.
Include award number, amount, period, and title. Discuss intellectual
merit and broader impacts of prior work, and list publications.

If no prior NSF support, state: "The PI has not received prior NSF
support."
